# F001. Technical Indicator + LightGBM Ranker

## Positioning

This is the repo's current executable baseline: technical features, a LightGBM scorer, and the existing risk/backtest rails. It is not glamorous, but it is the highest-confidence path for proving the stack works end-to-end.

## Reference Basis

- [LightGBM: A Highly Efficient Gradient Boosting Decision Tree](../../references/papers/lightgbm_neurips_2017.pdf) - core tabular ranker baseline
- [Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection](../../references/papers/0710.3742_bayesian_online_changepoint_detection.pdf) - regime prior if the technical score needs a shrinkage overlay

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Market Data and Features

Point-in-time data loading plus the shared feature builder used by most signal stacks.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - point-in-time market data loading
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - technical, lagged, and derived feature construction

### Cross-Sectional Ranker

The executable ranking path that turns scores into a tradable long/flat or top-k book.
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - LightGBM scoring model
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature matrix construction for the ranker

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use OHLCV, returns, and technical indicators from `src/features/builder.py`.
- Keep the data pipeline point-in-time and asset-aligned before the ranker sees it.
- Use the same risk features the current backtest and smoke paths already validate.

## Execution Path

- Rank assets cross-sectionally and turn the top scores into a long/flat or top-k book.
- Let the risk manager haircut exposure whenever volatility or drawdown rules trip.
- Replay the same score through offline backtest, synthetic matching, and paper-safe routing.

## Risk Controls

- Reject stale bars, duplicated candles, and any feature leak from the future.
- Keep the signal within the configured turnover budget.
- Stop trading if the health monitor or broker heartbeat fails.

## Validation Gates

- Compare directly against the current smoke and backtest paths.
- Check that the ranker still works after fees, slippage, and paper-safe gating are applied.
- Use the same assets and rebalance cadence when you compare this to F023 and F031.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if a simpler technical rule performs better after cost.
- Archive if the feature set leaks or becomes hard to maintain.
- Archive if the ranker cannot beat the baseline in both return and risk terms.

## Next Step

Keep this as the benchmark that every more complex candidate must beat.
